pattern: With a length capped so small, generating every binary string, filtering out the ones with two adjacent set bits, and keeping only those under the cost ceiling is fast enough to just do directly.
hints: The length is small enough that every one of the at most a few thousand binary strings of that length can be examined individually.
Reject any string that contains two consecutive '1' characters before even checking its cost.
For the remaining strings, add up the 0-based indices where a '1' appears and keep only the strings whose total does not exceed k.

edge_inputs.description: a small length with a modest cost limit
length one allows both possible strings
the smallest possible length and cost limit
the maximum allowed length with a generous cost limit
a cost limit of zero only allows the all-zero string and single leading one
no valid strings exist because even the cheapest non-adjacent arrangement exceeds the cost limit
a cost limit right at the boundary between excluding and including one more valid string

stress.description: every length from 1 to 12 with a range of cost limits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    k = rng.randint(0, n * (n - 1) // 2)
    return [n, k]
